hypothesis: Iter 76 = MOVE D, FIRST CONCRETE STEP: give the slow glycogen pools a real cold-model trajectory. This is a MEASUREMENT-blocked restart, not a new architecture. Iters 55-57 already put liver_glycogen / muscle_glycogen into the state vector as SetpointHead pools, but the only signal driving them was an indirect cohort window-mean delta ~6700x weaker than the glucose spec on the same arms — the pools never left `typical`. Cold-model distillation is the one signal strong enough to move a marker the bench scores but no real user measures (it already carries glucagon/ffa/ghrelin/leptin/acth/cortisol/bhb), and until now it COULD NOT distil glycogen because the cold ODE (knowledge/full_body.py) padded the two pools with the constants 100.0 / 400.0 — there was no trajectory to match. (1) SIMULATE THE POOLS. full_body.py now integrates liver + muscle glycogen as flux integrators (dGly/dt = synthesis - breakdown), tissue-specific by anatomy: the LIVER pool is the overnight-depleting fast store that defends blood glucose (glycogenolysis runs post-absorptively, suppressed by above-basal insulin, saturating as the pool empties — Cahill 2006: ~60 g of the ~100 g pool gone by a 24 h fast); the MUSCLE pool is rest-preserved (no hepatic G6Pase — muscle glycogen is spent locally during activity, not released to blood: Coppack 1989) and is the slow exercise-coupled reservoir the chronic-exercise north star (mitochondrial_capacity, still deferred) will eventually ride on. Verified in isolation: 12 h overnight fast depletes liver 100 -> 70.9 g with muscle flat; an exercise day with two Z2 bouts dents muscle 400 -> 337 g and refills toward 353. (2) DISTIL THEM IN ANCHORED MODE. Glycogen is a slow free-running LEVEL, so teacher-forced rate matching alone is blind to integrated pool depth — a model can score a perfect d/dt while its own level drifts. Anchored mode (iter 75, never yet run) = rate term (the synthesis/breakdown flux gradient through the strong gut-coupling path, the same pathway the learned GlycogenFluxHead reads) PLUS a short-horizon free-rollout absolute-level anchor (anchor_window=60, anchor_samples=8) that pins the depth. Switching the WHOLE distill set rate->anchored is deliberate and dual-purpose: it ALSO restores the level pin the hormones lost in iter 74, when adaptive cohort reweighting pulled budget off acth/cortisol/glucagon/ffa and their free-rollout levels drifted (acth 0.0755 -> 0.3618 was the canonical failure). (3) EXERCISE_DAY PROTOCOL. The cold protocol pool grows 8 -> 9 with a standard-meal day carrying two moderate (~Z2) bouts (45 min at 10:00, 30 min at 17:00) — the only protocol that drains muscle glycogen, so without it the muscle catabolic gate (activity-gated k_glyc_brk_M) would get zero distillation signal. It also broadens acute-exercise coverage (HR up, glucose down, FFA up, cortisol up, lactate up) toward the acute_z2_bout work. protocols-per-epoch 2 -> 4 (restoring the original 4-of-N default) so the now-9 pool — and exercise_day in particular — is sampled across the 10 phase-2 epochs. ATTRIBUTION (deliberate, single mechanism under test): glycogen does NOT yet feed back into the glucose / hepatic-output / ketone equations — the 19 observed-marker ODE is byte-identical to iter 75. The glycogenolytic glucose flux is still lumped inside `Hep`; splitting `Hep` into glycogenolytic + gluconeogenic components (so liver depletion legitimately drives fasting gluconeogenesis and ketosis, a mass-exact coupling) is the NEXT iter. This iter asks exactly one question: given a real cold-distill trajectory, do the slow pools track physiological depletion/repletion at all — the measurement iters 55-57 never had.
expectedEffect: Move D measurement iter — success = the slow pools track, NOT a gate pass (the glucose/hr gate is the acute-imputer surface, untouched here). Predictions: (1) dist_liver_glycogen / dist_muscle_glycogen (new per-marker distill telemetry) should be finite and fall over phase 2 as the GlycogenFluxHead learns the cold trajectory — liver should track the overnight-depletion arc (every day-long protocol exercises it), muscle the rest-preserved level plus the exercise_day depletion dent. This is the headline: the pools move off `typical` for the first time. (2) the hormone level drift from iter 74 should reverse now that anchored restores their level anchor: acth/cortisol/glucagon/ffa hold their levels rather than drifting (watch dist_acth, dist_cortisol, dist_glucagon, dist_ffa and anchor_skips). (3) overall weighted MAPE flat-to-better (~0.10-0.116; iter 74 landed 0.1097) — glycogen is not yet coupled into the observed markers, so it cannot move glucose/hr this iter by construction; any overall change comes via the hormone level pins. (4) textbook/verifier roughly flat-to-up as the hormone levels stabilise. Read dist_* per-marker, anchor_skips, and mode=2.0 in the cold_model_distillation telemetry to confirm anchored ran. Risks: (R1) ANCHORED MODE HAS NEVER RUN ON THE TRAINER. It keeps anchor_samples x anchor_window free-rollout graphs alive per protocol until one backward — a new memory consumer on top of 9 markers x 4 protocols/epoch. Calibration backward is gradient-checkpointed (the iter-67/68 OOM fix), but the anchor windows are not; if it OOMs it will do so early in phase 2 (epoch ~50) — monitor and, if so, drop anchor_samples 8->4 or protocols-per-epoch 4->3 and resubmit. (R2) exercise_day sampling is probabilistic: 4-of-9 ~ 44%/epoch over 10 phase-2 epochs ~ 4-5 exposures of the muscle catabolic arc — enough to start tracking, not saturate; if dist_muscle_glycogen stays flat, pin exercise_day into the per-epoch sample next iter. (R3) switching hormones rate->anchored changes their loss surface vs iter 74; intended to fix drift, but if an anchor window diverges it falls back to the rate half (logged via anchor_skips) — should be a strict improvement on the iter-74 rate-only drift, monitored. (R4) glycogen randomized pool sizes (LGly_b/MGly_b vary across patients) only diversify full_body episodes; cold-distill references use PatientParams() defaults, so the reference trajectory is deterministic and stable. (R5) liver depletion arc is front-loaded vs a strict linear Cahill rate (29 g by 12 h vs ~30 g implied) — within physiological spread, and the saturation term prevents it releasing glucose it no longer has. Runtime ~ unchanged (Phase 1 = 50, Phase 2 = 10; the added 9th protocol + 2 markers + anchor windows add modest phase-2 cost; n-epochs=80 is ignored when phased).
